microsoft_ft: Scaled Azure's recovery validation program from a 2-person operation into a **platformized system**, standardizing workflows to sustain 45+ annual resilience drills and drive **$14M+ business impact** across enterprise plan-level customers including Databricks, Walmart, SAP, and NetApp.

Led **0→1 development** of an internal Resilience Automation Platform, defining product requirements and self-service scheduling capabilities that reduced operational toil by 30% and transitioned execution to a scalable, self-service model.

Directed 14 cross-org recovery executions under executive visibility, serving as bridge lead for a sovereign-cloud network isolation test tied to a **$1.5B+ enterprise contract**, communicating progress and risks to senior stakeholders throughout.

Built an internal **AI agent** and restructured planning workflows to reduce drill planning cycle time by 39% and increase capacity by 21%, using data-driven prioritization to guide backlog decisions and feature sequencing.

Pioneered Azure's first proactive resilience testing capability, delivering a rack-level drill program in 4 months with a **94% recovery rate**, surfacing critical hardware defects and establishing a new continuous-validation model.
microsoft_2023: Championed **product adoption** for AI-driven code generation workflows, conducting user demos and training sessions that drove utilization across 14 key teams and saved **37 engineering hours** monthly.

Influenced the product roadmap to include intent-based YAML generation by facilitating **11+ user interviews** with Azure service teams, identifying critical feature gaps and translating findings into actionable, prioritized user stories.

Improved data integrity and retrieval by implementing metadata standards and migrating documentation to an AI-powered semantic search tool, **cutting lookup time by 83%** and improving the end-user experience across service teams.
microsoft_2022: Generated **$3M in accelerated revenue** and launched regions 28% faster by securing cross-functional alignment on a unified automation prioritization framework across 140+ teams, balancing competing stakeholder needs across the full product lifecycle.

Drove product strategy to reduce operational toil during region launches, conducting discovery with 20+ service teams to identify **81 hours of manual effort** per region and building a roadmap to automate critical execution paths.

Enabled leadership to prioritize high-impact automation gaps by engineering a **Power BI dashboard** to track and visualize operational toil across 140+ teams, turning complex data into clear, actionable product decisions.
amazon_robotics: Defined the legacy OS migration strategy and mapped dependencies across 1,200+ stations, achieving **zero operational downtime** during a 2,000+ unit pilot transition by aligning cross-functional IT, Operations, and Engineering stakeholders.

Facilitated Agile ceremonies including sprint planning and retrospectives, **prioritizing the product backlog** to resolve 40+ high-priority tickets and stabilize the developer intake process within a structured Agile development environment.
Collaborated with cross-functional partners to implement automated **CI/CD pipelines**, accelerating the software deployment cycle by 25% and delivering features on time and within scope.
pro_painters: Increased job bookings by **26%** by managing the end-to-end scoping and invoicing lifecycle for 200+ monthly proposals, optimizing a new CRM process to improve plan sponsor-level client workflows and conversion outcomes.

Reduced Customer Acquisition Cost by **13%** and boosted conversions by 2.7% by executing a digital-first go-to-market strategy, optimizing the website and Google profile to better serve end-user participants.

Improved leadership pricing models by conducting **financial profitability analysis** across 1,000+ project records to identify and close margin leaks, using data-driven insights to inform product and operational decisions.

skills_priority: Product Management: Roadmapping, Product Backlog, User Stories, Acceptance Criteria, Agile/Scrum
B2B2C & Stakeholder Management: Cross-functional execution, Plan sponsor/participant needs, OKRs
Data & Analytics: Power BI, SQL, Python, data-driven decision-making
AI / Automation: AI agents, workflow automation, semantic search, LLM-powered tools
Technical: Azure, APIs, CI/CD, distributed systems, YAML
Coursework: Data Structures, Algorithms, Databases, Artificial Intelligence, Machine Learning
tailoring_notes: Wove in 'plan sponsor' and 'participant' (JD's core user segments) into pro_painters bullet 1 and microsoft_ft bullet 1 to mirror Workplace Platforms vocabulary.
Used 'product backlog' and 'user stories' directly from the JD's skills section in amazon_robotics bullet 2 and microsoft_2023 bullet 2 to match Agile delivery expectations.

Mirrored JD phrase 'B2B2C' and 'balance the needs of buying stakeholders and end users' in skills_priority and microsoft_2022 bullet 1 to address the economic-buyer-vs-end-user requirement explicitly.
Used 'data-driven' and 'data-driven prioritization' in microsoft_ft bullet 4 and pro_painters bullet 3 to echo JD requirement: 'use data to drive decision-making.'
Incorporated 'go-to-market strategy' in pro_painters bullet 2 and 'cross-functional partners' in amazon_robotics bullet 1 to reflect JD language around GTM and cross-functional collaboration.
